currentUse: Active Roman Catholic church — Our Lady of Vilna Parish, Diocese of Worcester, now serving a Vietnamese Catholic community, with the Lithuanian interior substantially intact
summary: Aušros Vartų parish — known in English as Our Lady of Vilna, at 151/153 Sterling Street, Worcester, MA — is standing and remains an active Roman Catholic parish within the Diocese of Worcester; it was never sold out of Catholic hands. In December 2001 a Vietnamese Catholic community that had outgrown St. John Parish relocated to Our Lady of Vilna, which by then had few Lithuanian parishioners left, and the parish has served that community since. Current Sunday Masses (8:00 AM and 10:00 AM) are celebrated in Vietnamese, with weekday Masses Wednesday through Friday at 6:00 PM. The gothic-revival church, built around 1925, retains a strikingly Lithuanian interior — more Lithuanian inscriptions than nearly any other Lithuanian-American church, with saints' names labeled in Lithuanian behind the altar — and is described as the last church of this dedication in the USA; Lithuanian visitors are welcome. In October 2025 the parish celebrated its 100th anniversary with Bishop Robert McManus, and descendants of the Lithuanian founders joined the Vietnamese congregation for the celebration; diocesan press identifies the pastor as Fr. Peter Tam M. Bui. Our Lady of Vilna was not named in the Diocese of Worcester's 2025-2026 north Worcester parish-merger announcements, which involved other parishes.

developments.headline: Vietnamese Catholic community relocates to Our Lady of Vilna
developments.detail: The Vietnamese community came to Our Lady of Vilna in December 2001 after outgrowing space at St. John Parish, at a time when the Lithuanian parish had few parishioners left. The parish continued as a Catholic parish rather than being closed or sold.

sources.title: Our Lady of Vilna Parish celebrates 100 year anniversary
sources.publisher: The Catholic Free Press (Diocese of Worcester)

developments.headline: 100th anniversary celebrated jointly by Vietnamese congregation and Lithuanian descendants
developments.detail: The parish marked 100 years with a celebration attended by Bishop Robert McManus, who spoke of the gift of faith common to Lithuanians and Vietnamese. Descendants of the Lithuanian founders joined the Vietnamese community now worshipping there. Diocesan press names Fr. Peter Tam M. Bui as pastor, successor to Fr. Phuong Van Nguyen, and notes continued church renovations.

sources.title: Our Lady of Vilna Parish celebrates 100 year anniversary
sources.publisher: The Catholic Free Press (Diocese of Worcester)

developments.headline: Active Vietnamese-language Mass schedule; Lithuanian interior heritage preserved
developments.detail: Current listings show Sunday Masses at 8:00 AM and 10:00 AM in Vietnamese and weekday Masses Wednesday-Friday at 6:00 PM at 151 Sterling Street. Global True Lithuania documents that the interior remains staunchly Lithuanian — with more Lithuanian inscriptions than nearly every other Lithuanian-American church — that some Lithuanians still pray there, and that Lithuanian visitors are welcome.

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: The archive record's status "closed / diocese_closed" conflicts with present-day reality: the parish was never closed or sold by the diocese. Our Lady of Vilna Parish remains a canonically open, active Roman Catholic parish in the Diocese of Worcester, with regular Masses and a 100th-anniversary celebration in October 2025. What ended was the parish's Lithuanian character, not the parish itself — a more accurate framing would be "ethnic transition" rather than "diocese_closed". The archive's "transitioned to non-Lithuanian Catholic use by 2017" is confirmed directionally but refined: the Vietnamese community arrived in December 2001, so the transition was well underway 15+ years earlier than the archive's "by 2017" bound. The archive note "interior heritage substantially preserved" is confirmed (Global True Lithuania documents extensive Lithuanian inscriptions and saint imagery intact), as is the distinction from Worcester's Šv. Kazimiero (St. Casimir), a separate Lithuanian parish closed in 2009 and sold in 2011.
gaps: The Catholic Free Press article (2025-10-09) returned HTTP 403 on direct fetch; its facts (December 2001 Vietnamese arrival, Bishop McManus attendance, pastor Fr. Peter Tam M. Bui succeeding Fr. Phuong Van Nguyen, ongoing renovations) were obtained via search-engine excerpts of that article and were not confirmed on the rendered page — treat the pastor's name as probable pending direct confirmation with the parish or diocese. The diocesan parish page at worcesterdiocese.org also returned 403, so the diocese's own current pastor listing was not verified. Founding year: sources give slightly different anchors (parish effort begun 1912, church built ~1925, first Mass April 24, 1926, 100th anniversary celebrated October 2025); the exact canonical founding date was not established. Street address appears as both 151 and 153 Sterling Street across directories (rectory vs. church). No searched source names Our Lady of Vilna in the diocese's 2025-2026 restructuring announcements, but a comprehensive diocesan pastoral-plan document was not reviewed, so future restructuring exposure cannot be ruled out. No published sale price or buyer exists because the property was never sold.
